accepted_answers: coronary perforation with contained haematoma in the atrioventricular groove
localized AV groove haematoma causing pseudo-tamponade physiology
final_diagnosis._validation: Agent's final_diagnosis must be semantically equivalent to one of accepted_answers. Partial matches (e.g. 'coronary artery intramural hematoma complicating PCI') are acceptable if the core diagnosis is present.

specialist_findings._validation: Must contain sub-fields cardiac_analysis, vascular_analysis, systemic_analysis. Each must report total_cases, relevant_count, all_relevant_pmcids, top_10_cases, candidate_diagnosis_assessment, and domain_conclusion.

cardiac_supporting_cases._validation: At least 3 distinct PMCIDs from cardiac_cases.jsonl. Each entry MUST include a verbatim_excerpt (~first 150 chars of case_summary) AND a one-sentence justification. PMCIDs must exist in cardiac_cases.jsonl (NOT in vascular or systemic). At least 2 of the 3 gold_required PMCIDs must appear.

vascular_supporting_cases._validation: At least 3 distinct PMCIDs from vascular_cases.jsonl. Each entry MUST include a verbatim_excerpt AND a one-sentence justification. PMCIDs must exist in vascular_cases.jsonl (NOT in cardiac or systemic). At least 2 of the gold_expected PMCIDs should appear. The gold list includes both original selections and clinically valid alternatives (e.g. retroperitoneal hemorrhage cases with hemodynamic collapse and significant hemoglobin drops).

systemic_supporting_cases._validation: At least 3 distinct PMCIDs from systemic_cases.jsonl. Each entry MUST include a verbatim_excerpt AND a one-sentence justification. PMCIDs must exist in systemic_cases.jsonl (NOT in cardiac or vascular). At least 1 of the gold_expected PMCIDs should appear. The systemic domain is the weakest for this diagnosis — many cases match only on 'refractory hypotension' pattern, which a well-reasoning agent may correctly classify as partially relevant rather than fully relevant. Multiple valid case selections exist.

all_relevant_pmcids._validation: Non-empty list. Must include at least 2 of gold_required. Every PMCID must match PMC[0-9]+ format. Every PMCID must exist in cardiac_cases.jsonl.

cardiac_case_stats._first_last_validation: first_pmcid must be exactly 'PMC10601732' and last_pmcid must be exactly 'PMC11986323'. These prove the agent read the first and last lines of the file.

all_relevant_pmcids._validation: Non-empty list. Every PMCID must match PMC[0-9]+ format. Every PMCID must exist in vascular_cases.jsonl.

vascular_case_stats._first_last_validation: first_pmcid must be exactly 'PMC8081020' and last_pmcid must be exactly 'PMC11365614'. These prove the agent read the first and last lines of the file.

all_relevant_pmcids._validation: Non-empty list. Every PMCID must match PMC[0-9]+ format. Every PMCID must exist in systemic_cases.jsonl. Must include at least 1 of the gold_expected PMCIDs. Systemic domain serves primarily for differential exclusion — multiple valid case selections exist.

systemic_case_stats._first_last_validation: first_pmcid must be exactly 'PMC10629858' and last_pmcid must be exactly 'PMC5629885'. These prove the agent read the first and last lines of the file.

cross_domain_evidence_matrix._validation: Agent must include 3 candidate diagnoses as keys. Key names are matched SEMANTICALLY — e.g. 'coronary_intramural_hematoma' or 'intramural_hematoma' both match the 'intramural_haematoma' key; 'cardiac_tamponade' matches 'pericardial_tamponade'; 'nitrate_induced_hypotension' or 'pharmacologic_hypotension' match 'drug_induced_hypotension'. The agent's key equivalent to intramural_haematoma MUST have cardiac_evidence = 'supports'. The agent's key equivalent to pericardial_tamponade MUST have cardiac_evidence = 'contradicts'. For evidence values: exact match required for single-value oracle fields; any value in the array is acceptable for array-valued oracle fields.

most_discriminating_case._validation: PMCID must be one of accepted_pmcids. source_file must be cardiac_cases.jsonl. verbatim_excerpt must be a non-empty string starting with a plausible case narrative (e.g. 'A [age]-year-old...'). explanation must be non-empty.

weakest_domain._validation: The domain value must be 'vascular' or 'systemic' with a non-empty explanation. 'vascular' is correct by quantitative measure (fewest relevant_count). 'systemic' is also acceptable if the agent provides qualitative clinical reasoning — e.g. systemic evidence contradicts the diagnosis (SCLS causes hemoconcentration, not hemoglobin drop) making it evidentiarily weaker even if it has more classified-relevant cases. Any other domain value (e.g. 'cardiac') is incorrect.

excluded_diagnoses._validation: Agent must exclude at least 4 of the 6 required_exclusions. Each must include a reason. 'Coronary perforation with free rupture' is an exclusion — but 'contained micro-perforation leading to intramural haematoma' is the accepted MECHANISM and must NOT be excluded.

diagnostic_approach._validation: Must be a non-empty paragraph describing cross-domain analysis. Must reference all three domains (cardiac, vascular, systemic) — either by naming all three individually or by saying 'three domains' / '3 domains'. Must NOT claim to have used keyword matching or scripts for case classification (the task prohibits scripted/programmatic keyword matching). Mentioning 'sub-agents', 'multi-agent' delegation, or parallel analysis is ACCEPTABLE and expected in multi-agent mode — this is NOT the same as using scripts.
diagnostic_approach._required_mentions_logic: Must mention at least one variant from each group. The agent can say 'three domains' OR name 'cardiac', 'vascular', 'systemic' individually — both satisfy the requirement. The word 'cases' is NOT required since agents may naturally describe 'evidence', 'findings', 'data', or 'analysis' instead.

confidence._validation: Must be exactly 'high', 'medium', or 'low' (case-insensitive). All three are valid — the prompt explicitly offers all three options. An agent may reasonably output 'low' if it notes incomplete data (e.g. CT results not shown in the patient case).
